Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First I should recap the normative process. The process starts with Receive order. Then must be Check inventory next. After that depending on inventory: If sufficient Check inventory Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order. If insufficient Check inventory Cancel order Close order. Key constraints mentioned: Authorize payment must be after Check inventory and before packaging. Package items must be after authorization and before shipping. Ship order must be after packaging and never after a cancellation. Close order should be the final activity in all scenarios.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1: Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order This looks perfect for sufficient inventory. All steps in order. No violations here.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2: Receive order Check inventory Package items Authorize payment Ship order Close order Here after Check inventory they did Package items before Authorize payment. According to the norm Authorize payment must come before Package items. So this is a violation.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 3: Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Cancel order Ship order Close order after Check they did Authorize payment then canceled. Also after Cancel they did Ship order which is a big no-no. So two violations: Authorize payment before Cancel and Ship after Cancel. Definitely a violation.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 4: Receive order Check inventory Cancel order Close order This matches the insufficient inventory path. Receive Check Cancel Close. No fulfillment steps after Cancel. Looks good. No violation.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 5: Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Ship order Close order Here after Authorize payment they skipped Package items and went straight to Ship. The norm says Package must happen after Authorize and before Ship. So Ship without Package is a violation.

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: in Case 3 after Check inventory they did Authorize payment. That suggests that inventory was sufficient but then they canceled. The norm doesnt mention canceling after Authorize. So if inventory was sufficient you shouldnt cancel. Alternatively maybe the inventory was insufficient but they incorrectly authorized payment before canceling. Violation because Authorize should only happen if inventory sufficient. Also shipping after cancel wrong.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: So the violating cases are 2 3 5. Case 2 Package before Authorize. Case 3 Authorize before Cancel implying contradiction and Ship after Cancel. Case 5 missing Package between Authorize and Ship. Close order last in all cases no issue.
